diagnostic_tests.algorithm_summary: Re-extract the cell barcode at a range of start offsets around the expected position and measure the whitelist-match rate at each offset.
diagnostic_tests.interpretation: A sharp match-rate peak at a non-zero offset supports a barcode boundary shift; a strong diagnosis also shows restored whitelist rate and ideally improved molecule/cell recovery after re-extraction.
diagnostic_tests.limitations: Cannot distinguish a boundary shift from a wrong whitelist if no offset restores the match rate.
diagnostic_tests.outputs: Whitelist-match rate as a function of offset; the offset that maximises the match rate.
diagnostic_tests.purpose: Detect a barcode/UMI extraction boundary shift by scanning candidate offsets.
diagnostic_tests.title: Barcode offset scan

diagnostic_tests.algorithm_summary: Match extracted barcodes against several candidate chemistry whitelists and compare match rates.
diagnostic_tests.interpretation: A markedly higher match rate against a different whitelist supports a wrong-chemistry/whitelist cause.
diagnostic_tests.limitations: Requires the true chemistry's whitelist to be among the candidates.
diagnostic_tests.outputs: Per-whitelist match rate; the best-matching chemistry.
diagnostic_tests.purpose: Identify whether a different chemistry whitelist explains the barcodes.
diagnostic_tests.title: Alternative-whitelist scan

diagnostic_tests.algorithm_summary: Compare observed read lengths and segment positions against the spec's read_structure (R1 length vs cell barcode + UMI, expected cycles, orientation).
diagnostic_tests.interpretation: Any mismatch (e.g. R1 shorter than barcode + UMI) supports a read-configuration cause.
diagnostic_tests.limitations: Detects layout mismatches, not whether an otherwise-valid configuration was mis-processed.
diagnostic_tests.outputs: A list of mismatches between observed and expected read configuration.
diagnostic_tests.purpose: Check that the observed read layout matches the chemistry's expected barcode/UMI/insert geometry.
diagnostic_tests.title: Read-configuration audit

diagnostic_tests.algorithm_summary: Attempt barcode/UMI extraction with R1 and R2 roles swapped and compare whitelist-match rates.
diagnostic_tests.interpretation: A much higher match rate in the swapped orientation supports an R1/R2 swap.
diagnostic_tests.limitations: Only distinguishes a swap, not other configuration errors.
diagnostic_tests.outputs: Whitelist-match rate in normal vs swapped orientation.
diagnostic_tests.purpose: Detect whether read 1 and read 2 were swapped.
diagnostic_tests.title: R1/R2 swap test

diagnostic_tests.algorithm_summary: Scan each read for the expected adapters/handles and classify by which are present and where; the Nanopore path implements this per-read classification.
diagnostic_tests.interpretation: Elevated adapter-only / short-insert fractions support adapter-dimer / short-cDNA causes.
diagnostic_tests.limitations: Read-level configuration fractions are not the same as physical library fractions.
diagnostic_tests.outputs: Per-configuration read fractions (full-length, adapter-only, short-insert, unclassified).
diagnostic_tests.purpose: Classify each read by its adapter/handle configuration to quantify full-length, adapter-only, short-insert, and TSO-proximal reads.
diagnostic_tests.title: Adapter-configuration classification

diagnostic_tests.algorithm_summary: Scan long reads for TSO-derived motifs occurring internally (away from read ends); the Nanopore path implements this as the tso_concatemer check.
diagnostic_tests.interpretation: An elevated internal-TSO fraction supports a long-read concatemer/fusion cause; it is distinct from short-read TSO read-through.
diagnostic_tests.limitations: Cannot by itself establish cross-sample molecule mixing.
diagnostic_tests.outputs: Internal-adapter fraction and per-read internal-motif positions.
diagnostic_tests.purpose: Detect internal TSO-derived motifs in long reads indicating concatemers or fused molecules.
diagnostic_tests.title: Internal TSO scan (long-read)

diagnostic_tests.algorithm_summary: Recompute called cells across a range of calling thresholds and inspect where the count stabilises relative to the knee.
diagnostic_tests.interpretation: A large increase in called cells when the threshold is relaxed supports overly strict calling; a flat curve points upstream (input/capture).
diagnostic_tests.limitations: Cannot create cells that were never captured; distinguishes calling strictness from upstream loss only when molecules exist near the knee.
diagnostic_tests.outputs: Called-cell count as a function of threshold.
diagnostic_tests.purpose: Assess how called-cell count varies with the cell-calling threshold.
diagnostic_tests.title: Cell-calling sensitivity analysis

diagnostic_tests.algorithm_summary: Re-extract barcodes/UMIs at the offset found by the offset scan and recompute whitelist rate and downstream molecule/cell recovery.
diagnostic_tests.interpretation: Restored whitelist rate and improved recovery confirm a barcode boundary shift and demonstrate computational recoverability.
diagnostic_tests.limitations: Full recovery requires that the shift is the dominant defect.
diagnostic_tests.outputs: Whitelist rate and recovery before vs after counterfactual re-extraction.
diagnostic_tests.purpose: Confirm a barcode boundary shift by re-extracting at the corrected offset and measuring recovery.
diagnostic_tests.title: Counterfactual barcode re-extraction

computational_actions: Barcode offset / alternative-whitelist scan and re-extraction
impacts: Fewer called cells and reduced statistical power
Potentially biased population representation if loss is non-random
missing_evidence: Viable-cell input / cells-loaded from wet-lab metadata (needed to separate input loss from processing loss)
Raw FASTQ for counterfactual barcode re-extraction
sequencing_actions: Additional sequencing only helps if the library is sound but shallow
issues.summary: Far fewer cells were called than the declared target. Many mechanisms can cause this, from barcode-processing errors to insufficient input; the target must be a compatible target type before comparing.
issues.title: Low called-cell recovery
wetlab_actions: Confirm viable-cell input and capture efficiency; rerun if input was insufficient
issues.what_this_issue_cannot_explain: It does not by itself establish cross-sample contamination or expression-fidelity bias; those require their own evidence. A low cell count with a healthy whitelist rate points upstream (input/capture/calling), not to barcode processing.

computational_actions: Classify adapter configurations and quantify recoverable vs discarded reads
missing_evidence: Physical library QC (e.g. fragment sizing) to relate sequenced-read fractions to library composition
sequencing_actions: Additional sequencing recovers depth when the usable library is intact
issues.summary: A large share of sequencing was spent on reads that carry little usable single-cell information (adapter-only, short-insert, TSO-proximal, or tail-artifact reads).
issues.title: Low informative-read yield
wetlab_actions: Bead cleanup or library rebuild if adapter-dimer / short-insert load is severe
issues.what_this_issue_cannot_explain: It does not directly establish a specific called-cell target collapse; relate it to cell recovery only via reads-in-cells depth. A physical library dimer percentage is not the same as the sequenced-read percentage.

computational_actions: Barcode offset scan, alternative-whitelist scan, R1/R2 swap test, then re-extract
impacts: Few reads assigned to valid barcodes
Collapse in called cells and per-cell metrics
missing_evidence: Raw FASTQ needed for offset / whitelist / swap scans
issues.summary: Reads cannot be assigned to valid barcodes/UMIs at the expected rate, starving every downstream per-cell metric.
issues.title: Barcode / UMI recovery failure
issues.what_this_issue_cannot_explain: A whitelist/extraction failure does not imply the wet-lab library is bad; it is usually a read-processing/configuration problem and is often computationally recoverable.

computational_actions: Classify configurations; separate short-read TSO read-through from long-read internal TSO motifs
issues.summary: Sequenced molecules deviate from the expected architecture (short/empty inserts, TSO-proximal reads, adapter-only reads, or long-read internal motifs).
issues.title: Abnormal library structure
issues.what_this_issue_cannot_explain: Structural abnormality alone does not quantify lost cells or prove cross-sample mixing; a short-read TSO read-through and a long-read internal TSO motif are different events and must not be conflated.

metrics.description: Total reads (or read pairs) produced by the run before quality filtering.
metrics.label: Total reads

metrics.description: Reads passing the run/basecaller quality filter.
metrics.label: Pass reads

metrics.denominator: reads passing quality / total reads produced
metrics.description: Fraction of produced reads that pass the quality filter.
metrics.label: Pass-read fraction

metrics.denominator: reads with an extractable barcode / reads examined
metrics.description: Fraction of reads from which a candidate cell barcode could be extracted at the expected position (before whitelist matching).
metrics.label: Barcode-extractable fraction

caveats: Denominator differs between producers; do not merge with barcode.valid_fraction unless the definition matches.
metrics.denominator: reads with exact whitelist match / reads with an extracted barcode
metrics.description: Fraction of reads whose extracted barcode matches the chemistry whitelist exactly, before error correction.
metrics.label: Barcode whitelist-match fraction

aliases.denominator: reads with a barcode matching the whitelist after correction
aliases.label: Valid Barcodes

metrics.denominator: reads with a valid (corrected) barcode / reads examined
metrics.description: Fraction of reads assigned to a valid barcode after whitelist matching and error correction.
metrics.label: Valid-barcode fraction

metrics.description: Fraction of valid barcodes that required one-mismatch error correction to reach the whitelist.
metrics.label: Barcode-corrected fraction

metrics.description: Fraction of reads from which a UMI of the expected length could be extracted at the expected position.
metrics.label: UMI-extractable fraction

metrics.description: Fraction of reads carrying a structurally valid UMI (correct length, not a homopolymer/low-complexity artifact).
metrics.label: Valid-UMI fraction

metrics.description: Fraction of reads matching the expected full-length architecture (both flanking adapters/handles in the expected orientation).
metrics.label: Full-length-proxy fraction

caveats: A physical adapter-dimer percentage in the library is not equal to the sequenced-read percentage unless cluster-generation bias is modelled.
metrics.description: Fraction of reads composed essentially of adapter/handle sequence with no meaningful insert (adapter dimers).
metrics.label: Adapter-only fraction

metrics.description: Fraction of reads whose inferred insert is shorter than expected, causing adapter read-through into the read.
metrics.label: Short-insert fraction

metrics.description: Fraction of short-read cDNA reads that begin with template-switch-oligo-derived sequence, indicating short/empty cDNA products. Strand-specific: the 10x 3' TSO (AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTACATGGG) is distinct from its reverse complement TSO_RC (CCCATGTACTCTGCGTTGATACCACTGCTT).
metrics.label: TSO-at-RNA-read-start fraction (short-read)

metrics.description: Fraction of long reads containing internal adapter/TSO-derived motifs mid-read, indicating concatemers or fused molecules. This is a distinct diagnostic event from short-read TSO read-through at the read start.
metrics.label: Internal-adapter fraction (long-read)

metrics.description: Fraction of reads ending in a poly-G run (two-colour Illumina no-signal) or an equivalent no-signal tail. A supporting signal of read-past-end / signal decay, not an independent root cause.
metrics.label: Poly-G tail fraction

metrics.description: Fraction of reads that do not match any expected library configuration.
metrics.label: Unclassified-read fraction

metrics.description: Fraction of reads mapping to the reference genome.
metrics.label: Genome-mapped fraction

metrics.description: Fraction of reads with a primary alignment.
metrics.label: Primary-alignment fraction

metrics.description: Fraction of reads with no acceptable alignment.
metrics.label: Unmapped fraction

metrics.description: Fraction of reads with supplementary (chimeric/split) alignments; elevated values can indicate fused/concatemeric molecules.
metrics.label: Supplementary-alignment fraction

metrics.description: Fraction of reads confidently assigned to a gene/feature.
metrics.label: Gene-assigned fraction

metrics.description: Fraction of reads confidently assigned to a transcript/isoform.
metrics.label: Transcript-assigned fraction

metrics.description: Number of distinct genes detected across the dataset.
metrics.label: Unique genes detected

metrics.description: Number of distinct isoforms detected across the dataset.
metrics.label: Unique isoforms detected

metrics.description: The declared cell target for the library. Its meaning depends on the target type (cells loaded, expected recovered cells, ...); do not compare it to called cells across incompatible target types.
metrics.label: Target cell count
metrics.target_dependency: Interpretation depends on the input-manifest cell_target.target_type.

metrics.description: Number of barcodes called as real cells by the cell-calling step.
metrics.label: Called cell count

metrics.description: Called cells divided by the target cell count. Only meaningful when the target type is compatible with called cells.
metrics.label: Target attainment
metrics.target_dependency: Undefined unless cell_target.target_type is expected_recovered_cells or expected_called_cells.

metrics.description: Fraction of valid-barcode reads that belong to barcodes called as cells.
metrics.label: Fraction reads in cells

metrics.description: Mean number of reads per called cell.
metrics.label: Mean reads per cell

metrics.description: Median number of genes detected per called cell.
metrics.label: Median genes per cell

metrics.description: Median number of UMIs (unique molecules) per called cell.
metrics.label: Median UMIs per cell

metrics.description: Descriptive measure of how cleanly the barcode-rank (knee) plot separates cells from background; a poor separation implies ambiguous cell calling.
metrics.label: Barcode-rank knee separation

metrics.description: Fraction of reads that are duplicates of already-observed (barcode, UMI, gene) molecules; a measure of how completely the library has been sampled.
metrics.label: Sequencing saturation

metrics.description: Fraction of reads that are PCR/optical duplicates.
metrics.label: Duplicate fraction

recovery_actions.description: The signal can be corrected in silico from existing data (e.g. re-extract barcodes at a corrected offset, re-run cell calling) without new sequencing or a new library.
recovery_actions.label: Computationally recoverable

recovery_actions.description: Some information can be rescued computationally, but a fraction is unrecoverable from the current data.
recovery_actions.label: Partially recoverable

recovery_actions.description: The existing library is sound but under-sequenced for the goal; more sequencing recovers the signal.
recovery_actions.label: Recoverable by additional sequencing

recovery_actions.description: The molecular library itself is compromised; recovery needs a new library from retained material.
recovery_actions.label: Requires library rebuild

recovery_actions.description: The defect originates upstream of library prep (e.g. cell input); recovery needs a new experiment.
recovery_actions.label: Requires experiment rerun

recovery_actions.description: Available evidence does not determine recoverability.
recovery_actions.label: Unknown / insufficient evidence

references.assay_context: 10x Chromium 3' gene expression (v3/v3.1): 16 nt cell barcode, 12 nt UMI, TSO = AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTACATGGG
references.title: Chromium Single Cell 3' Reagent Kits v3 / v3.1 User Guide

references.assay_context: Short-read single-cell analysis (Estimated Number of Cells, Valid Barcodes, Reads Mapped to Genome, Sequencing Saturation, ...)
references.title: Cell Ranger web summary metric definitions

references.assay_context: Long-read single-cell analysis report (estimated cells, full-length reads, adapter configuration, gene/transcript assignment)
references.title: EPI2ME wf-single-cell workflow documentation

references.assay_context: Long-read 10x 3' single-cell; barcode/UMI recovery and full-length transcript assignment on Nanopore
references.title: High throughput error corrected Nanopore single cell transcriptome sequencing (ScNaUmi-seq / Sicelore)

cell_recovery_relationship.note: A boundary shift collapses whitelist matching and therefore valid barcodes, which directly starves cell calling.

expression_fidelity_relationship.note: If barcodes are re-extracted correctly, expression is typically unaffected; residual mis-extraction adds noise.

root_causes.mechanism: Barcode/UMI extraction is offset by a few bases from the true position (e.g. unexpected upstream trimming or a wrong offset), so extracted barcodes rarely match the whitelist and valid-barcode reads collapse.
recommended_actions: Re-extract barcodes/UMIs at the scanned offset and re-run downstream steps.

sample_integrity_relationship.note: A boundary shift does not move molecules between samples; it is an extraction geometry error.

root_causes.title: Barcode boundary shift

cell_recovery_relationship.note: If R1 cannot yield a valid barcode+UMI, essentially no barcodes are recovered and cell calling fails.

expression_fidelity_relationship.note: Some mismatches (e.g. swapped reads) are fully recoverable; wrong cycle counts may be unrecoverable.

root_causes.mechanism: The read layout does not match the chemistry: R1 shorter than cell barcode + UMI, R1/R2 swapped, wrong barcode or UMI length, wrong sample-index cycles, wrong chemistry, or unexpected upstream trimming. A top-tier direct cause of cell-recovery collapse.
recommended_actions: Audit the read layout against the spec; if only extraction is wrong, re-extract; if cycles are wrong, additional sequencing or a rerun may be required.

sample_integrity_relationship.note: A configuration error affects all reads uniformly rather than mixing samples.

root_causes.title: Read-configuration mismatch

cell_recovery_relationship.note: With the wrong whitelist, valid barcodes are near zero and no cells are called.

expression_fidelity_relationship.note: Fully recovered once the correct whitelist is applied.

root_causes.mechanism: Analysis used the wrong chemistry/whitelist, so the correct barcodes never match the loaded whitelist.
recommended_actions: Scan alternative chemistry whitelists and re-run with the best-matching one.

sample_integrity_relationship.note: Whitelist choice does not mix samples.

root_causes.title: Wrong chemistry or barcode whitelist

cell_recovery_relationship.note: Reduces usable molecules per cell; only collapses called-cell count if the informative yield falls far enough to starve cell calling.

expression_fidelity_relationship.note: Adapter-only reads are discarded rather than mis-quantified.

root_causes.mechanism: Adapter-dimer and short-insert molecules consume sequencing capacity and produce adapter-only or read-through reads. Directly relevant to low informative yield. Note: a physical library dimer percentage does not equal the sequenced-read percentage unless cluster-generation bias is modelled.
recommended_actions: Classify adapter configurations; if the usable library is sound, additional sequencing recovers depth; severe dimer load may need a cleanup or rebuild.

sample_integrity_relationship.note: Dimers do not move molecules between samples.

root_causes.title: Adapter dimers or short inserts

cell_recovery_relationship.note: Lowers informative yield per cell; does not by itself explain a large cell-count collapse.

expression_fidelity_relationship.note: Short products can bias 3' coverage and gene detection.

root_causes.mechanism: Reverse transcription produced short or empty cDNA, so reads begin with TSO-derived sequence (short-read) or fail the full-length architecture (long-read).
root_causes.title: Short or empty cDNA products

cell_recovery_relationship.note: Fewer viable cells loaded directly caps how many can be called.

expression_fidelity_relationship.note: Recovered cells are still faithfully quantified.

root_causes.mechanism: Too few viable cells were loaded, so few real cells can be recovered regardless of downstream processing.

sample_integrity_relationship.note: Low input does not mix samples.

root_causes.title: Insufficient viable-cell input

cell_recovery_relationship.note: Poor capture/RT reduces molecules per cell, weakening the knee and lowering called cells.

expression_fidelity_relationship.note: Low capture can bias which transcripts are detected.

root_causes.mechanism: Inefficient droplet/partition capture or reverse transcription yields few molecules per cell and a weak cell/background separation.
root_causes.title: Poor partition capture or RT

cell_recovery_relationship.note: Low yield lowers depth and reads-in-cells; whether cells collapse depends on how shallow the data is.

expression_fidelity_relationship.note: Very shallow libraries can distort per-cell expression.

root_causes.mechanism: Low cDNA/library yield gives shallow, ambient-dominated data with few reads in cells.
root_causes.title: Low cDNA or library yield

cell_recovery_relationship.note: The molecules exist but are excluded by the calling threshold, so relaxing it recovers cells.

expression_fidelity_relationship.note: Recovered cells are unchanged in their expression.

root_causes.mechanism: The cell-calling threshold is too strict, discarding real cells that sit near the knee.

sample_integrity_relationship.note: A threshold choice does not mix samples.

root_causes.title: Overly strict cell calling

cell_recovery_relationship.note: Tail artifacts are usually trimmed and rarely explain a large cell-count collapse.

expression_fidelity_relationship.note: If untrimmed, tails can reduce alignment/assignment rates.

root_causes.mechanism: Sequencing reads past the insert end or suffers signal decay, producing poly-G (two-colour) or no-signal tails.
root_causes.title: Read-past-end or signal decay

cell_recovery_relationship.note: Fused/concatemeric reads reduce cleanly assignable molecules but do not directly collapse cell calling.

expression_fidelity_relationship.note: Mis-split concatemers can distort per-molecule assignment.

root_causes.mechanism: Long reads contain internal TSO-derived motifs from concatemerised or fused molecules, distinct from short-read TSO read-through at the read start.

sample_integrity_relationship.note: A fused read can join molecules from different barcodes; treat as a per-read structural artifact, not established cross-sample hopping.

root_causes.title: Long-read TSO concatemer or fused read

signals.description: The number of called cells is much lower than the declared cell target.
signals.label: Called-cell count far below the declared target

signals.description: A small fraction of valid-barcode reads belong to called cells, suggesting ambient/background dominance or poor capture.
signals.label: Low fraction of reads in cells

signals.description: The barcode-rank curve does not show a clean separation between cells and background.
signals.label: Barcode-rank plot lacks a clear knee

signals.description: Few reads carry a barcode matching the chemistry whitelist.
signals.label: Barcode whitelist match is low

signals.description: Few reads yield an extractable barcode or UMI at the expected offsets.
signals.label: Barcode/UMI cannot be extracted at the expected position

signals.description: An elevated fraction of reads are adapter/handle-only (adapter dimers).
signals.label: Adapter-only reads are elevated

signals.description: An elevated fraction of reads read through into the adapter, implying short inserts.
signals.label: Short-insert / adapter read-through elevated

signals.description: Short-read cDNA reads begin with TSO-derived sequence, implying short/empty cDNA products. Distinct from internal long-read TSO motifs.
signals.label: TSO-derived sequence at the start of a short-read cDNA read

signals.description: Long reads contain internal adapter/TSO-derived motifs mid-read, implying concatemers or fused molecules.
signals.label: Internal adapter/TSO motifs in long reads

signals.description: An elevated fraction of reads end in poly-G (two-colour no-signal) tails. This is a SUPPORTING signal of read-past-end or signal decay, not an independent root cause.
signals.label: Poly-G / no-signal tails elevated

signals.description: Few long reads match the expected full-length architecture.
signals.label: Low full-length-proxy fraction

signals.description: Reads map to the genome at a high rate but are assigned to genes/features at a low rate.
signals.label: Genome mapping high but gene assignment low
